# Mutation-testing equivalence file
#
# Documented-equivalent mutations to suppress from the survivor count.
# Each non-blank, non-comment line:
#
#     <file>:<line>:<op>:<original>:<mutated>    # optional comment
#
# Match is exact on all five fields, except line may be M-N for a range
# and op/original/mutated may each be `*` to match anything. When a
# mutant's fingerprint matches, it's reported as IGNORED rather than
# SURVIVED, and the effective kill rate is computed as
# killed / (total - ignored).
#
# Adding entries is a deliberate act:
#   - Verify the mutation is *truly* equivalent (no observable behaviour
#     change for any reasonable input the function sees).
#   - Cite the equivalence class in the trailing comment so the next
#     reviewer can re-verify.
#
# Equivalence classes (extend in docs/MUTATION-TESTING.md):
#   OPT  - early-return optimisation guard (correctness unaffected;
#          mutation only causes wasted work)
#   WGT  - composite-score weight constant where the literal ±1
#          perturbation can't shift bucketed output
#   LZT  - loop bound reading zero-initialised tail (e.g. unused
#          char_counts[] slots)
#   FAB  - function/local-as-boolean: any non-zero replacement is
#          still truthy and observably equivalent
#   ALG  - allocator-internal sizing/growth literal that changes how
#          memory is acquired but not the algorithm's output
#   SAN  - sanitizer-detectable memory bug (buffer overrun, read-past-
#          end, free-of-uninitialised). Survives our -O0 test build
#          because macOS's allocator doesn't fault; would be killed by
#          a re-run with `make CFLAGS='-O0 -g -fsanitize=address,undefined'`
#          (see docs/MUTATION-TESTING.md for the ASAN run target)
#
# Start empty. Earn each entry by triaging a real survivor.

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# src/complexity.c — Round 1 triage
# ------------------------------------------------------------
src/complexity.c:34:rel:<:<=          # LZT: char_counts[256] is read past array end; the >0 guard at L35 makes any value at that index unobservable (UB-tolerant on typical stacks)
src/complexity.c:52:const:1:0         # OPT: run_length init is overwritten on the first loop iteration's else branch (i=0 always differs from '\0')
src/complexity.c:167:const:10:11      # WGT: score cap unreachable — max composite is ~3.1 given the current 0.3/0.25/0.2/0.15/0.1 weights and component ranges
src/complexity.c:167:const:10:9       # WGT: same — even a 9.0 cap exceeds the max achievable composite

# Round 2 — pattern_complexity equivalents
src/complexity.c:76:const:2:1         # OPT: `if (len < 2)` → `if (len < 1)`. For len=1 the outer loop never runs (slen starts at 2 > 1) so both paths return 0.0 via the total_substrings==0 guard at L127.
src/complexity.c:93:const:1:2         # FAB: `int is_unique = 1` used purely as boolean (only checked via `if (is_unique)`); any non-zero value is observably identical.
src/complexity.c:105:const:64:65      # ALG: initial substr_capacity literal; affects realloc cadence, not output.
src/complexity.c:105:const:64:63      # ALG: same.
src/complexity.c:105:const:2:3        # ALG: realloc growth factor (2x → 3x); changes capacity progression, not unique-substring count.
src/complexity.c:110:const:1:2        # ALG: `malloc(slen + 1)` → `malloc(slen + 2)`; one extra byte is harmless tail.
src/complexity.c:110:arith:+:-        # SAN: `malloc(slen + 1)` → `malloc(slen - 1)`; heap overflow on the strncpy+null-terminator that follows. Killed by ASAN.
src/complexity.c:110:const:1:0        # SAN: `malloc(slen + 1)` → `malloc(slen)`; one-byte overflow on the null terminator. Killed by ASAN.
src/complexity.c:122:rel:<:<=         # SAN: free-loop `i < substr_count` → `i <= substr_count`; frees one uninitialised slot past the populated range. Killed by ASAN.
